org.arakhne.neteditor.fig.figure.edge.symbol

Class EdgeSymbol

  • java.lang.Object
    • org.arakhne.neteditor.formalism.AbstractPropertyTooler
      • org.arakhne.neteditor.fig.figure.edge.symbol.EdgeSymbol
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    Serializable, PropertyNames
    Direct Known Subclasses:
    AbstractFillableEdgeSymbol, ArrowEdgeSymbol

    public abstract class EdgeSymbol
extends AbstractPropertyTooler
implements PropertyNames
    This class is the base class for the symbols that will be paint at the ends of edges.

    The symbol is associated to an edge and attached to a Anchor at a given point. In other words, the attach position is the point where the symbol will be connect to a port.

    Version:
    17.0 2013-10-23 21:03:01
    Author:
    Stéphane GALLAND
    See Also:
    Serialized Form
    Maven Group Id:
    org.arakhne.neteditor
    Maven Artifact Id:
    neteditor-fig

    • Constructor Detail

      • EdgeSymbol

        public EdgeSymbol(EdgeFigure<?> edge)
        Construct a new Symbol.
        Parameters:
        edge - the edge that contains this arrow.

      • EdgeSymbol

        public EdgeSymbol()
        Construct a new Symbol.

    • Method Detail

      • setEdge

        public void setEdge(EdgeFigure<?> edge)
        Set the edge that contains this symbol.
        Parameters:
        edge - the edge that contains this symbol.

      • getEdge

        public EdgeFigure<?> getEdge()
        Set the edge that contains this symbol.
        Returns:
        the edge that contains this symbol.

      • getBounds

        public Rectangle2f getBounds()
        Return the rectangle in which the symbol is.
        Returns:
        a rectangle in which the symbol is.

      • setGeometry

        public final void setGeometry(float x_begin,
               float y_begin,
               float x_end,
               float y_end)
        Change the attach point and the the rotating angle from the given edge coordinates.
        Parameters:
        x_begin - x coordinate from the edge point where the symbol is.
        y_begin - y coordinate from the edge point where the symbol is.
        x_end - x coordinate from the edge point where the symbol is not.
        y_end - y coordinate from the edge point where the symbol is not.

      • contains

        public boolean contains(float x,
               float y)
        Reply true if the given Point is in this symbol.
        Parameters:
        x - is the coordinate that may be contained in this symbol.
        y - is the coordinate that may be contained in this symbol.
        Returns:
        true if p is in this symbol, false otherwise.
        See Also:
        hit(float, float, float)

      • hit

        public boolean hit(float x,
          float y,
          float epsilon)
        Reply true if given coords may correspond to a position of the mouse cursor that permits to hit this figure. This function should take into account the shape of the figure, and not only the bounds as for contains(float, float)
        Parameters:
        x - horizontal coord.
        y - vertical coord.
        epsilon - is the allowed error when testing the hits.
        Returns:
        true if the point (x,y) is in this Fig.
        See Also:
        contains(float, float)

      • paint

        public final void paint(ViewGraphics2D g)
        Paint the arrow.
        Parameters:
        g - the graphical context in which the arrow must be paint.

      • paintSymbol

        protected abstract void paintSymbol(ViewGraphics2D g,
               Shape2f shape)
        Invoked to draw the property symbol.
        Parameters:
        g - is the graphics context.
        shape - is the symbol shape.

      • getSymbolShape

        protected abstract Shape2f getSymbolShape(float x,
                     float y,
                     float angle)
        Compute and replies the shape of the symbol.
        Parameters:
        x - is the position of the symbol anchor.
        y - is the position of the symbol anchor.
        angle - is the angle of the symbol.
        Returns:
        the shape.

      • getLineColor

        public Color getLineColor()
        Reply the color of lines. By default this method take the color from the edge.
        Returns:
        the color of the lines.

      • getFillingColor

        public Color getFillingColor()
        Reply the color of filling area. By default this method take the color from the edge.
        Returns:
        the color of the filled areas.

        public Map<String,Object> getProperties()
        Replies the properties of this component.
        Returns:
        the properties, never null.

      • getSupportedProperties

        public Map<String,Class<?>> getSupportedProperties()
        Replies the names and the types of all the properties that are understood by this component.
        Returns:
        the properties' specification, never null.

        public void setProperties(Map<String,Object> properties)
                   throws IOException
        Set the properties of the model object, except the UUID.

        properties - are the properties of this model object, except the UUID.
